description_long: This object is a heavily damaged and partially deconstructed airplane fuselage, with its metallic ribbed structure and corrugated panels exposed. The surface is predominantly a dull gray, with scattered patches of rust, dirt, and some orange and brown elements, possibly remnants of wiring or insulation. The wings appear to be missing or broken off, leaving only the central body and some attached components. The fuselage is flattened and fragmented, with jagged edges and torn sections, suggesting it has been through a crash or extensive dismantling. Some mechanical parts and debris are visible along the sides, and the overall form is low and wide, lacking any visible cockpit or tail section. The object is surrounded by small scattered fragments, further emphasizing its derelict state.
description: A damaged, flattened airplane fuselage with exposed ribbed metal panels, rust, and debris, missing wings and tail. The surface is mostly gray with scattered orange and brown elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long, ribbed fuselage with jagged, torn edges and scattered debris along the bottom.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the width of the fuselage, visible internal structure, and some orange and brown components.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with more visible mechanical parts and a clearer look at the fragmented, open structure.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, showing the ribbed panels and more scattered debris around the object.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: No wings present; only the central fuselage remains, with no visible high-wing, mid-wing, or low-wing configuration.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible on the remaining structure.
attribute_tail_design: Tail section is missing; no vertical or horizontal stabilizers are present.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; possible remnants of wheel mounts along the sides.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Cockpit section is not visible; forward fuselage is missing or destroyed.
